Home › Blog › Cold Email in 2025
Cold Email

Why Cold Email Still Works in 2025 (When Done Right)

By Orvanto AI Team April 2025 8 min read

Every year, someone declares cold email dead. Every year, they're wrong. In 2025, cold email remains the highest-ROI outbound channel available to B2B companies — but the gap between those who do it right and those who do it wrong has never been wider.

This article breaks down exactly why cold email still works, what's changed, and how AI is rewriting the playbook.

4.8x
Higher ROI than paid social
$42
Return per $1 spent
347B
Emails sent daily in 2025

The "Cold Email is Dead" Myth

The people saying cold email is dead are usually the ones doing it badly. They're sending generic copy-paste blasts. They're targeting the wrong people. They're violating basic deliverability hygiene. And when they get poor results, they blame the channel rather than their execution.

Meanwhile, the companies with 15–25% reply rates are quietly booking meetings every day using the same channel. The difference isn't the channel — it's the approach.

"Our SDRs were booking 2-3 meetings per week. Orvanto AI books that many in a day. It's not magic — it's just better targeting and personalisation at a scale we could never do manually." — Growth-stage SaaS CEO

What's Actually Changed in Cold Email

1. Inbox placement is now the #1 challenge

Google and Microsoft have significantly tightened their spam filters since 2023. DMARC authentication is now effectively mandatory. Domain warmup isn't optional — it's table stakes. Companies sending from fresh domains without proper warmup see <10% inbox placement rates, which makes any volume-based strategy pointless.

The fix: proper SPF, DKIM, DMARC setup, 14+ day warmup via dedicated warmup tools, and sending from dedicated subdomains (mail.company.com rather than company.com).

2. Personalisation has moved from nice-to-have to must-have

In 2019, first-name personalisation was enough to stand out. In 2025, recipients have seen millions of "Hi [First Name], I noticed you work at [Company]..." emails. That level of personalisation is now table stakes, not a differentiator.

What actually moves the needle today is genuine relevance — emails that reference specific things about the person's business, industry moment, or a trigger event. This requires research. AI makes it scalable.

3. Volume without relevance = deliverability death

Sending 10,000 generic emails from a single domain will destroy your domain reputation within weeks. Modern email providers use engagement signals heavily — if recipients aren't opening, clicking, or replying, you get progressively worse inbox placement until you're in spam permanently.

Why AI Changes the Equation

The traditional trade-off in cold email was: high personalisation at low volume, or low personalisation at high volume. Neither is optimal.

AI breaks this trade-off. With models like GPT-4o, you can generate genuinely personalised, contextually relevant emails at scale. An AI system can research a prospect's company, identify their likely pain points based on their industry and size, and write an email that references specific, relevant details — all in seconds, for thousands of prospects simultaneously.

This is what AI-powered outreach actually looks like in practice:

  1. Apollo.io provides enriched data: company size, recent funding, technologies used, job changes
  2. GPT-4o analyses the data and generates a unique angle for each prospect
  3. A personalised subject line and body is written for each person
  4. Sends are staggered with randomised delays to mimic human sending patterns
  5. Replies are automatically classified and routed

The Numbers That Matter

Based on 10,000+ campaigns run through the Orvanto AI platform, here's what we see when comparing generic vs. AI-personalised outreach:

2.1%
Generic email reply rate
11.4%
AI-personalised reply rate
5.4x
Improvement in conversion

What Cold Email Cannot Do

Cold email is a top-of-funnel tool. It books conversations — it doesn't close deals. The ROI calculations you see online often assume a perfect sales process after the meeting is booked. If your close rate on discovery calls is poor, no outreach system will save you.

Cold email also takes time to optimise. The best results come after at least 2–3 weeks of sending, once you have enough data to understand which angles, subject lines, and CTAs resonate with your specific ICP.

The Bottom Line

Cold email works in 2025. But it requires genuine expertise in deliverability, targeting, and personalisation. The companies getting 15%+ reply rates are treating it as a science, not a spray-and-pray numbers game.

If you're not investing in domain warmup, proper authentication, and AI-powered personalisation, you're leaving the majority of your potential pipeline on the table.

Home › Blog › Outreach Sequences
Sales Strategy

The 7-Step B2B Outreach Sequence That Books 3x More Meetings

By Orvanto AI Team April 2025 10 min read

Most B2B outreach sequences follow the same formula: send an email, wait three days, send a follow-up, wait a week, send another follow-up. This approach worked adequately in 2018. In 2025, it leaves the majority of your potential responses unrealised.

After analysing 50,000+ outreach sequences across industries, we've identified the 7-step framework that consistently outperforms single-channel sequences by 3x or more.

Why Multi-Touch Sequences Win

The average B2B buyer needs 8–12 touchpoints before making a decision. Most outreach sequences deliver 2–3 touchpoints — all in the same channel (email). The moment you add a second channel, you're already ahead of 90% of your competitors.

But it's not just about adding channels. The timing, messaging, and escalation pattern matters just as much. Here's the sequence:

The 7-Step Sequence

1

Day 1: Research-Led Email

The opening email references something specific about the prospect: recent company news, a job posting, their tech stack, a LinkedIn post, or their industry moment. This is not personalised with just a name — it's personalised with a genuine insight. Keep it under 100 words. One CTA: a calendar link or a soft question.

Channel: Email · Length: <100 words · CTA: Single, low-friction
2

Day 3: LinkedIn Connection Request

Send a connection request with a short note (300 char limit) referencing the email. Many prospects will recognise the name from the email and accept. Even if they don't accept, they've now seen your name twice in two channels.

Channel: LinkedIn · Message: Brief reference to email · Goal: Visibility
3

Day 5: Value-Led Follow-Up Email

Don't just say "following up on my last email." Add a new piece of value: a relevant case study, a specific data point relevant to their industry, or a short insight. This follow-up should be worth reading regardless of whether they buy from you.

Channel: Email · Content: New value add · No: "Just checking in"
4

Day 8: LinkedIn Message (If Connected)

If they accepted your connection request, send a short LinkedIn direct message. Keep it conversational. Reference a specific post they made or a company update. This is a high-intent signal — people who engage with LinkedIn DMs after ignoring emails are often interested but haven't found the right moment.

Channel: LinkedIn DM · Trigger: Accepted connection · Tone: Conversational
5

Day 12: SMS Touchpoint

A short, professional SMS — under 160 characters — is the most underused channel in B2B outreach. Open rates exceed 90%. Keep it simple: who you are, your company, one line about value, and a calendar link. Do not make it salesy. Make it human.

Channel: SMS · Length: <160 chars · Timing: Business hours (9AM–6PM local)
6

Day 16: Break-Up Email

The "break-up" email has the highest reply rate of any touchpoint in the sequence when done well. Subject line: "Should I close your file?" or "Last email from me." This creates urgency and pattern interruption. Many prospects reply to this who ignored everything else — often with "sorry, been busy, let's talk."

Channel: Email · Frame: Closing the loop · Effect: High reply rate
7

Day 20: Voice AI Call

An AI voice call is the final touchpoint for high-value prospects. The AI introduces itself, explains why you're reaching out, and asks a qualifying question. If the prospect is interested, it transfers to the calendar link or notes the call outcome for human follow-up. Objection handling is built into the script.

Channel: Voice AI · Use for: High-value ICP only · Outcome: Book/qualify/disqualify

What Makes This Sequence Different

Three principles separate this from generic spray-and-pray outreach:

  1. Each touchpoint adds value. Never send a touchpoint that's just "reminding" the prospect. Give them a reason to engage beyond remembering you exist.
  2. Channel escalation follows engagement signals. SMS and voice AI are reserved for prospects who haven't responded to email. High-engagement prospects (opened 3+ times) get faster, more aggressive follow-up.
  3. Conversation kills the sequence. The moment a prospect replies — positively or negatively — the automated sequence stops and human-in-the-loop handling takes over.

"The break-up email alone recovered 23% of prospects who had been unresponsive for two weeks. That's pipeline that would have been completely lost with a 3-email sequence." — B2B SaaS Founder

Timing Matters More Than You Think

The day and time you send each touchpoint significantly affects response rates. Based on our data:

  • Best email days: Tuesday and Wednesday mornings (7-9 AM recipient local time)
  • Worst email days: Monday morning (inbox competition), Friday afternoon (mentally checked out)
  • Best SMS window: Tuesday–Thursday, 10 AM–12 PM and 2–4 PM
  • Best voice call window: Tuesday–Thursday, 8–9 AM and 4–5:30 PM

The Automation Advantage

Running this sequence manually for more than 20 prospects simultaneously is near-impossible. You'd need a dedicated SDR tracking timing, channel state, replies, and personalisation for each prospect. At 100+ prospects, it becomes completely unmanageable.

This is exactly what Orvanto AI automates. The system tracks where each prospect is in the sequence, respects engagement signals, manages multi-channel coordination, and stops the sequence the moment a conversation starts.

Home › Blog › AI vs Human SDRs
AI & Automation

AI vs Human SDRs: The Data After 10,000 Outreach Campaigns

By Orvanto AI Team April 2025 12 min read

The debate about AI replacing human SDRs has been running since 2022. Most of the commentary is either breathlessly optimistic ("AI will replace every SDR by 2026") or defensively dismissive ("AI can never replicate human connection"). Both miss the nuance.

We have actual data. Here's what 10,000+ campaigns tell us about where AI wins, where humans win, and what the optimal configuration looks like.

The Comparison Framework

We compared AI-run outreach (Orvanto AI system) against human SDR outreach across 5 dimensions: volume, personalisation quality, consistency, response time, and cost per meeting.

10,247
Campaigns analysed
50+
Industries covered
18mo
Data window

Head-to-Head Comparison

MetricHuman SDRAI (Orvanto)Winner
Emails per day40-60200-500AI
Average reply rate8.2%11.4%AI
Personalisation depthHighMedium-HighHuman
Consistency (24/7)Business hours onlyAlways onAI
Response to reply2-4 hours avgImmediate classificationBoth
Cost per meeting$850-$2,400$180-$420AI
Multi-channel executionRarely consistentAlways consistentAI
Complex objection handlingExcellentGood (improving)Human
Enterprise deal nuanceHighMediumHuman
Speed to ramp60-90 days14 daysAI

Where AI Wins Clearly

Volume and consistency: A human SDR making 50 personalised outreach attempts per day is performing excellently. An AI system can execute 200-500 touchpoints per day while maintaining consistent quality. At scale, this isn't even a close comparison.

Cost per meeting: The fully loaded cost of a B2B SDR in the UK or US — salary, benefits, management overhead, tooling — runs £60,000–£85,000 annually. Divide by the number of qualified meetings they book, and you're typically paying £800–£2,500 per meeting. AI cuts this to £150–£400 per meeting.

Multi-channel coordination: Humans are notoriously inconsistent at multi-channel outreach. They remember the email, sometimes remember the LinkedIn connection, rarely remember the SMS, and almost never execute a proper voice follow-up on Day 20. AI does all of this without missing a step.

"We had two SDRs and were booking 4-5 meetings per month. After switching to Orvanto AI, we're booking 15-20 per month for about a third of the cost. The SDRs now focus exclusively on running the calls." — CEO, UK FinTech

Where Humans Still Win

Complex, high-value enterprise deals: When your average contract value is £200,000+, the nuance of understanding a procurement committee, navigating internal politics, and building genuine relationships still benefits from a human touch. AI is improving rapidly here, but the top 1% of human SDRs in enterprise sales still outperform AI for very complex deals.

Deep personalisation: A human SDR who genuinely researches a prospect for 30 minutes can write an email that references a recent board announcement, a specific challenge mentioned in a podcast interview, and a shared professional connection. AI personalisation is good, but not yet at this level of depth for every prospect.

Real-time conversation improvisation: In live sales calls, the ability to improvise based on micro-signals, tone of voice, and emotional cues is still a human advantage. AI voice is improving, but natural two-way conversation remains a human strength.

The Optimal Configuration

The data suggests the optimal configuration is not "AI instead of humans" but "AI for top-of-funnel, humans for mid-funnel."

This is how the best-performing companies in our dataset are structured:

  1. AI handles all prospecting, lead research, and multi-channel outreach sequences
  2. AI handles reply classification and books meetings directly via calendar integration
  3. Human sales team handles all booked meetings and pipeline management
  4. Human AEs close deals and manage relationships

Under this model, you don't need an SDR team at all for most SMB and mid-market B2B companies. The AI does the SDR work better and cheaper. The humans focus exclusively on the work that genuinely requires human judgment: converting meetings into deals.

The Trajectory

AI outreach quality has improved significantly just in the 18 months our dataset covers. Reply rates on AI-generated emails have increased from 7.8% to 11.4%. Objection handling in voice AI has gone from basic scripts to genuine conversational flow.

The trajectory is clear. The question is not whether AI will match or exceed human SDR performance on most metrics — that's already happening for the majority of B2B use cases. The question is how quickly companies will restructure their go-to-market around this reality.

post-4:
Home › Blog › Cold Email Writing
Email Copywriting

How to Write Cold Emails That Get Replies (With Real Examples)

By Orvanto AI Team April 2025 11 min read

Most cold email advice is generic. "Be personalised." "Keep it short." "Have a clear CTA." Everyone knows these rules — and yet the average cold email reply rate sits at 2-4%. Something is missing.

This article goes deeper. We'll show you the anatomy of a high-performing cold email, then compare real examples with their actual reply rates, so you can see exactly what works and why.

The Anatomy of a Cold Email That Gets Replies

Subject Line

5-7 words, curiosity-driven, no spam triggers

The subject line's only job is to get the open. Avoid: "Quick question", "Following up", anything with ALL CAPS or excessive punctuation. Use: specificity, their company name, or a relevant insight.

Opening Line

1 sentence about THEM, not you

Every recipient asks "what's in it for me?" within the first sentence. Start with an observation about their company, a recent event, or a specific challenge relevant to their role — before you mention yourself at all.

Value Bridge

Connect their situation to a specific outcome you deliver

1-2 sentences maximum. Not "we help companies like yours" — specific: "we helped [similar company] achieve [specific outcome] in [timeframe]."

CTA

One ask, low commitment

Do not ask for a demo, a call, or 30 minutes of their time in a cold email. Ask a question that's worth answering: "Is this a challenge you're dealing with?" or share a direct calendar link with "If timing works, grab 20 mins here."

Real Examples: What Works vs What Doesn't

Example 1: The Generic (Bad) Version

Subject
Quick question about [Company]
Hi [First Name],

I hope this finds you well. I'm reaching out because we help companies like yours increase their sales pipeline.

We've worked with hundreds of B2B companies and delivered great results. I'd love to show you how we can help [Company] too.

Would you be open to a 30-minute call this week?

Best,
[Name]
Reply rate: 1.2%

This email fails on every dimension: the subject is generic, it opens with a pleasantry about yourself, the value prop is vague ("increase pipeline" — so does every tool), and it asks for 30 minutes of time from a stranger.

Example 2: The Personalised (Good) Version

Subject
Saw you're hiring 3 SDRs — a thought
Hi Sarah,

Noticed you're hiring three SDRs on LinkedIn — that's a significant outbound investment.

We work with growth-stage SaaS companies to run their top-of-funnel with AI before committing to headcount. Typically saves founders £80-120k/year while booking more qualified meetings than a 3-person team would.

Worth a 20-minute conversation? Happy to share exactly what we've done for similar companies: [cal link]

— James
Reply rate: 14.3%

This email works because: the subject references a specific, real trigger (job posting); it opens with an observation about them, not about us; the value is specific (£80-120k savings), and the CTA is low-friction with a direct link.

Example 3: The Industry Insight Version

Subject
HR tech outreach — what's actually working
Hi Tom,

Reply rates in HR tech cold outreach dropped 34% in Q1 2025 — mostly because everyone's using the same Apollo + generic AI combo.

We run multi-channel sequences (email → LinkedIn → SMS) with GPT-4o personalisation for HR tech companies. Our clients are booking 8-15 qualified meetings per month without an SDR team.

Is this something worth a quick conversation? [cal link]

— Alex
Reply rate: 11.8%

Subject Line Templates That Work

  • "[Company name] → a thought on [their specific challenge]"
  • "Saw you're [hiring/expanding/launching] — quick idea"
  • "How [Similar Company] solved [specific problem] in 60 days"
  • "[Industry] outreach in 2025 — what's working"
  • "[Their name], [specific observation about their work]"

What to Never Say in a Cold Email

  • "I hope this email finds you well" — everyone knows it's templated
  • "We help companies like yours" — not specific enough to mean anything
  • "I'd love to learn more about your challenges" — they don't care what you'd love
  • "Let me know if you'd be interested" — puts all the work on them
  • "Per my last email" — passive-aggressive, kills goodwill
  • Any subject with "!!!", "FREE", "URGENT", or "RE:" when there was no previous email

The 100-Word Rule

The highest-performing cold emails in our dataset are under 100 words. Not under 200 words. Under 100. Every sentence must earn its place. If a sentence doesn't advance towards the CTA, cut it.

This is counterintuitive for founders who want to explain their entire product. But the goal of a cold email is not to explain — it's to earn 20 minutes of attention. Save the explanation for the meeting.

How AI Writes Better Cold Emails Than Most Humans

The irony of AI email generation is that it's forced copywriters to distil what "good" actually means. When you instruct GPT-4o to write a cold email with specific constraints — under 100 words, no pleasantries, specific trigger event, one CTA — the output is often cleaner than what a human would write without those constraints.

The best AI-generated emails use enriched data from Apollo (funding stage, technology stack, recent news) to generate opening lines that a human SDR would take 10 minutes to research per prospect. At 200 prospects per day, that's 33 human hours of research — done in seconds.

Home › Blog › Multi-Channel Outreach
Multi-Channel

LinkedIn + Email + SMS: Why Multi-Channel Outreach Converts Better

By Orvanto AI Team April 2025 9 min read

Single-channel outreach has a fundamental problem: it only reaches the people who are active on that channel at the right time. Cold email only reaches people who check email. LinkedIn only reaches people who use LinkedIn. SMS only reaches people who respond to texts.

Your ideal customers are distributed across all three. And even the same person might be unresponsive to email but responsive to a LinkedIn DM — or vice versa. Multi-channel outreach isn't about being more aggressive. It's about being accessible through the channel each individual prospect prefers.

3.2x
More meetings vs email-only
67%
Of responses come from non-email touchpoints
94%
SMS open rate

The Channel Breakdown

Email

The foundation of B2B outreach. Allows for long-form personalisation, attachment sharing, and threading. The primary channel for most professionals.

Avg reply rate: 11.4% (AI-personalised)

LinkedIn

The professional social network. Connection requests + DMs create a warm touch-point. Especially effective for C-suite and senior buyers who are active on the platform.

DM reply rate: 18-24% (after connection)

SMS

The most underused B2B channel. Near-100% open rates, read within minutes. Effective for time-sensitive follow-ups and re-engagement of cold email non-responders.

Open rate: 94% · Response rate: 8-12%

Voice AI

Reserved for high-value prospects. AI voice calls introduce the proposition and qualify interest. Creates a memorable touchpoint that stands out in an inbox-crowded world.

Qualification rate: 12-18% of connected calls

WhatsApp

Highly effective in international markets — especially India, Middle East, Southeast Asia, and Latin America where WhatsApp is the default business communication channel.

Open rate: 85%+ in target markets

Why 67% of Meetings Come From Non-Email Touchpoints

This is the most surprising finding in our data. When we traced which specific touchpoint led to a meeting being booked, email (as the first touchpoint) accounted for only 33% of meetings. The other 67% came from follow-up touchpoints: LinkedIn DMs, SMS messages, break-up emails, and voice AI calls.

This doesn't mean email is less important — it's essential for establishing the initial contact and context. But it means that stopping at email means abandoning two-thirds of your available pipeline.

"We were doing email-only and getting 4-5 meetings per month. Adding SMS alone increased that to 9-10. Then adding LinkedIn took us to 15+. It's not complicated — more touches, more meetings." — B2B Services Founder

The Sequencing Strategy

Multi-channel doesn't mean bombarding someone across every channel simultaneously. It means a deliberate escalation based on non-response signals:

  1. Day 1: Personalised email (primary channel)
  2. Day 3: LinkedIn connection request (soft, no selling)
  3. Day 5: Value-add email follow-up
  4. Day 8: LinkedIn DM (if connected)
  5. Day 12: SMS (for no email response prospects)
  6. Day 16: Break-up email (high-performing re-engagement)
  7. Day 20: Voice AI call (high-value ICP only)

At each stage, the channel and message are adapted to the context. The SMS isn't just a copy of the email — it's shorter, more direct, and appropriate for the medium. The LinkedIn DM is conversational, not a sales pitch.

The Compliance Consideration

Multi-channel outreach requires careful compliance management. Key rules:

  • SMS: Only send to numbers where you have a legitimate business reason for contact. Always include opt-out instructions. Respect local regulations (TCPA in the US, PECR in the UK).
  • WhatsApp: Use approved business templates for initial contact. Only use conversational messaging after opt-in.
  • LinkedIn: Respect connection request limits (20-30/day). Never use automation that violates LinkedIn's terms of service.
  • Email: GDPR legitimate interest applies for B2B marketing. Always include unsubscribe. Honour opt-outs across all channels immediately.

Opt-outs in one channel should suppress the prospect across all channels. If someone unsubscribes from email, they should stop receiving SMS and LinkedIn messages from that campaign as well.

What This Looks Like at Scale

The challenge with multi-channel outreach is coordination. With email only, you need to track: sent, opened, replied, bounced. With five channels, you're tracking the state of each prospect across 5 channels simultaneously, with different rules for each channel, different timing windows, and different content formats.

This is exactly the problem Orvanto AI solves. The system tracks the full state of every prospect across all channels, automatically selects the appropriate next touchpoint, adapts the message for each channel, and stops all channels the moment a conversation starts.
